name: West Enclave Merger Corp.
business: West Enclave Merger Corp. is a blank check company formed to effect a merger, share exchange, or similar business combination with one or more operating businesses. It intends to focus on identifying a high-quality business that operates in Latin America or a US-based business positioned to benefit from the economic interconnection between the United States and Latin America, particularly Mexico. The company has not yet selected any specific target business and has generated no revenues.

exposures.sentence: These may include companies operating in consumer, retail and branded goods platforms benefiting from demographic expansion and rising household formation; digital-assets and technology-enabled financial services driving rapid growth in financial services; tourism and tourism related services that leverage the increased inflow of tourism; industrial and logistics services supporting nearshoring and supply-chain relocation; and infrastructure and real assets with capabilities to leverage technologies across regions.

catalysts.sentence: We will have up to 21 months from the closing of this offering to consummate a business combination.

tamClaims.claim: Equity market capitalization to GDP ratios in Latin America are significantly lower than in the US: 27% in Mexico, 38% in Brazil in 2025 versus 222% in the US.
tamClaims.sentence: We also note that the ratio of total equity market capitalization to gross domestic product across Latin America remains significantly below that of the United States, estimated to be 27% and 38% in Mexico and Brazil, respectively, in 2025, compared to an estimate of 222% in the United States in the same year, in line with ten-year averages of approximately 31%, 47% and 180% for Mexico, Brazil and the United States, respectively.
